title: calamintha officinalis subsp. nepeta
intro: Calamintha officinalis subsp. nepeta, commonly known as Nepeta calamintha or Calamint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues and relieving pain. The health benefits of Calamintha officinalis subsp. nepeta include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. It can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
